tool_args.label: MTA Regular Board Meeting - July 27, 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Regular Board Meeting of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority and its agencies held July 27, 2022.

payload.description: MTA and MTA Agencies Regular Board Meeting held at 2 Broadway, New York, NY. Meeting included public comment, Chair Lieber's remarks, CFO financial plan presentation, CBD Tolling update, Capital Program amendment approval, TMRB establishment, and procurement approvals.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Regular Board Meeting 2 Broadway New York, NY 10004 Wednesday, July 27, 2022 10:00 a.m.

tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting - September 21, 2022
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Board Meeting at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY on September 21, 2022.

payload.description: MTA Board Meeting with procurement and real estate action items including Penn Station Access property acquisitions, Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 property acquisition, TMRB establishment approval, and Grand Central Terminal retail licensing program modification.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004 Wednesday, 9/21/2022 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM ET

tool_args.label: Traffic Mobility Review Board Established
tool_args.raw_text: The Board approved the establishment of the Traffic Mobility Review Board (TMRB) and the appointment of TMRB members.

payload.description: MTA Board approved establishment of the Traffic Mobility Review Board (TMRB) pursuant to NY CLS PAL, Section 553-k, and appointment of TMRB members. Board members Mack and Borelli voted in opposition; Velez abstained.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 7. CENTRAL BUSINESS TOLLING PROGRAM - APPROVAL OF TRAFFIC MOBILITY REVIEW BOARD
the Board approved the establishment of the Traffic Mobility Review Board

tool_args.label: 2020-2024 Capital Program Amendment Approved
tool_args.raw_text: Board approved amendment to the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program increasing total funding to $55.442 billion.

payload.description: Board approved proposed amendment to the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program that increases total funding to $55.442 billion and authorizes MTA to submit the $52.116 billion Capital Program Review Board (CPRB) portion to the CPRB.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA 2020-2024 CAPITAL PROGRAM AMENDMENT
the Board approved the proposed amendment to the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program that increases the total funding to $55.442 billion

tool_args.label: Ridership at 60% of pre-COVID levels
tool_args.raw_text: ridership since Omicron has returned more slowly than expected—at just 60% of pre-COVID numbers, when prior estimates had us by the end of this year closing in on 80-85%

payload.claim_text: ridership since Omicron has returned more slowly than expected—at just 60% of pre-COVID numbers, when prior estimates had us by the end of this year closing in on 80-85%
payload.description: Chair Lieber stated that MTA ridership after Omicron is at 60% of pre-COVID levels, slower than the 80-85% previously projected for end of 2022.

evidence_refs.source_quote: ridership since Omicron has returned more slowly than expected—at just 60% of pre-COVID numbers

tool_args.label: Operating deficit $500M larger in July Plan
tool_args.raw_text: the operating deficit in the July Plan is projected to be $500 million larger and it will come sooner than previously anticipated

payload.claim_text: the operating deficit in the July Plan is projected to be $500 million larger and it will come sooner than previously anticipated
payload.description: Chair Lieber stated that the operating deficit in the July Financial Plan is $500 million larger than prior estimates.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the operating deficit in the July Plan is projected to be $500 million larger

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access - Property Acquisition/Eminent Domain
tool_args.raw_text: MTA seeking approval of Determination and Findings under Eminent Domain Procedure Law for acquisition of property interests in the Bronx, Queens, and Westchester for the Penn Station Access Project.

payload.description: Board approved Determination and Findings under the NY Eminent Domain Procedure Law for acquisition of fee interests and permanent easements in the Bronx, Queens, and Westchester for the Penn Station Access Project. Project provides direct rail access to Penn Station from southern Connecticut, Westchester, and East Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, with four new Metro-North stations at Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-op City. Estimated time frame for property needs: late 2023 through mid-2024.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Penn Station Access Project will provide direct rail access to Penn Station-New York and Manhattan's west side
DETERMINATION AND FINDINGS PURSUANT TO ARTICLE 2 OF THE NEW YORK EMINENT DOMAIN PROCEDURE LAW
The Project will make various infrastructure improvements to Amtrak's existing Hell Gate Line and will construct four (4) new Metro-North stations

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 - Property Acquisition
tool_args.raw_text: Authorization to acquire permanent and temporary easements through negotiated agreement or eminent domain for Phase 2 of the Second Avenue Subway.

payload.description: Board authorization to acquire permanent and temporary easements for construction of Phase 2 of the Second Avenue Subway. The new subway tunnel will run from 105th Street to 125th Street, then west along 125th Street to just past Lenox Avenue. Easements primarily needed for support of excavation, ground support, and improvement work.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In connection with the construction of Phase 2 of NYCT's Second Avenue Subway Project
tunnel that will run from 105th Street to 125th Street, and then west along 125th Street to just past Lenox Avenue

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 - Property Acquisition
tool_args.raw_text: Authorization to acquire permanent and temporary easements through negotiated agreement or eminent domain for Phase 2 of the Second Avenue Subway.

payload.description: Board authorization to acquire permanent and temporary easements for construction of Phase 2 of the Second Avenue Subway. Easements primarily needed for support of excavation, ground support, and improvement work.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In connection with the construction of Phase 2 of NYCT's Second Avenue Subway Project
MTA will endeavor to acquire all of the required Easements through negotiated agreements

tool_args.label: Metro-North Upgrade Automotive Fuel System Project
tool_args.raw_text: Acquisition of real property at 4 Fisher Lane, White Plains, NY for the Metro-North Upgrade Automotive Fuel System Project.

payload.project_name: Metro-North Upgrade Automotive Fuel System Project
payload.description: Design and construction of automotive fueling stations with above ground systems to replace existing facilities at Harmon Yard, Brewster North Yard, and North White Plains Yard. Requires acquisition of property at 4 Fisher Lane, White Plains, adjacent to North White Plains Yard. Current locations have 30-year-old underground tanks and fueling systems in need of replacement.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North's AFS Project, addresses design and construction of automotive fueling stations with above ground fueling systems to replace existing facilities at Harmon Yard, Brewster North Yard and North White Plains Yard
the North White Plains site requires the acquisition of property at 4 Fisher Lane

tool_args.label: Grand Central Terminal Retail Licensing Program - Grand Central Madison Extension
tool_args.raw_text: Modification to the Grand Central Terminal Retail Licensing Program to include Grand Central Madison's retail concourse.

payload.project_name: Grand Central Terminal Retail Licensing Program Modification
payload.description: Board approved extension of the Grand Central Terminal Short Term License Program to include Grand Central Madison's retail concourse and LIRR mezzanine. Short-term license agreements will be used to engage pop-up retail until a long-term retail master tenant is secured.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In Grand Central Madison, the same short-term license agreements will be used to engage and activate pop-up retail along the Madison Concourse, and the LIRR mezzanine below
MTA Real Estate seeks authorization to amend the current Grand Central Terminal Retail Licensing Program to include Grand Central Madison

tool_args.label: Bronx Bus Network Redesign - Successful Rollout
tool_args.raw_text: Chair Lieber celebrated the successful rollout of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign, stating most customers are reacting positively.

payload.description: Chair Lieber celebrated the successful rollout of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign at the July 27, 2022 Board meeting. Stated the bus system in the Bronx remained virtually unchanged for decades and most customers are reacting positively. 225 employees volunteered to be Bronx Bus Ambassadors talking to customers across the borough as the program launched.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Chair Lieber stated that he wants to take a moment to celebrate one of MTA's successes of late—the successful rollout of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign
225 employees who volunteered to be Bronx Bus Ambassadors, talking to customers across the borough as the program launched
